# copilot-instructions-template

> Lean/Kaizen GitHub Copilot template for VS Code projects. It scaffolds model-pinned agents, reusable skills, hooks, MCP config, workspace identity files, and update protocols into a user's repository.

## Start here

- [.github/copilot-instructions.md](.github/copilot-instructions.md): Main always-loaded instruction file. Core methodology and guardrails.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Machine entry point. Trigger phrases and canonical setup or update pointers.
- [MODELS.md](MODELS.md): Single source of truth for agent model assignments and thinking-effort guidance.
- [SETUP.md](SETUP.md): Remote bootstrap protocol for consumer projects.
- [UPDATE.md](UPDATE.md): Remote update, backup restore, and factory restore protocol.
- [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md): Per-version migration registry.

## Model strategy

> **Single source of truth**: [MODELS.md](MODELS.md) — edit there, then run
> `bash scripts/sync/sync-models.sh --write` to propagate to agent files.

| Surface | Recommended model | Thinking Effort | Role |
|---------|-------------------|-----------------|------|
| `setup.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Medium | Template lifecycle (setup, updates, backup restore, factory restore) |
| `coding.agent.md` | GPT-5.3-Codex | High | Implementation, refactoring, multi-step coding |
| `organise.agent.md` | GPT-5.1 | Medium | Subagent-only structural cleanup, file moves, path repair, and directory layout normalization |
| `review.agent.md` | GPT-5.4 | High | Deep review, debugging, architectural analysis |
| `fast.agent.md` | Claude Haiku 4.5 | Low | Quick lookups and lightweight edits |
| `audit.agent.md` | GPT-5.4 | High | Read-only health check and security audit (structural, OWASP, secrets, supply chain) |
| `explore.agent.md` | Claude Haiku 4.5 | Low | Fast read-only codebase exploration and Q&A |
| `extensions.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Medium | VS Code extension management and profiles |
| `researcher.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | High | Online/offline research and documentation |
| `commit.agent.md` | GPT-5.1 | Low | Stage, commit, push, tag, and release management |
| `planner.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | High | Read-only planning, scoping, and implementation sequencing |
| `docs.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Medium | Documentation generation, guides, and user-facing explanations |
| `debugger.agent.md` | GPT-5.4 | High | Root-cause diagnosis, failure triage, and minimal fix-path analysis |

> **Thinking Effort** is a per-user, per-model setting in the VS Code model picker.
> Low = fast/cheap, Medium = balanced, High = deep reasoning. See `MODELS.md`
> "Thinking Effort Guide" for rationale. No per-agent override exists in
> `.agent.md` frontmatter — these are configuration recommendations.

## Skills

Skill inventory changes over time.
Use `.copilot/workspace/operations/workspace-index.json` as the canonical source for exact skill names and counts.

High-signal examples:

- `extension-review`: audit VS Code extensions against detected stack signals
- `test-coverage-review`: audit test coverage and recommend local tests plus CI workflows
- `tool-protocol`: search before building automation
- `skill-management`: discover and manage skills on demand
- `mcp-management`: configure external tool access
- `plugin-management`: discover and manage agent plugins

## Hooks

Hook orchestration is configured in `.github/hooks/copilot-hooks.json` and mirrored in
`template/hooks/copilot-hooks.json`. Canonical script inventory and counts live in
`.copilot/workspace/operations/workspace-index.json`.

Current lifecycle events:

This table is a quick summary. For exact executable wiring, use `.github/hooks/copilot-hooks.json`.

| Event | Primary script(s) | Purpose |
|-------|-------------------|---------|
| SessionStart | `session-start`, `pulse --trigger session_start` | Initialize session context and heartbeat state |
| UserPromptSubmit | `pulse --trigger user_prompt` | Detect explicit heartbeat or retrospective prompts |
| PreToolUse | `guard-destructive` | Block dangerous terminal commands |
| PostToolUse | `post-edit-lint`, `pulse --trigger soft_post_tool` | Lint edits and debounce heartbeat soft triggers |
| PreCompact | `save-context`, `pulse --trigger compaction` | Preserve state before context compaction |
| Stop | `scan-secrets`, `pulse --trigger stop` | Secret scan and retrospective gate |
| SubagentStart | `subagent-start` | Inject governance context for subagents |
| SubagentStop | `subagent-stop` | Log subagent completion |

## Subagent orchestration

Each agent declares an `agents:` allow-list in `.github/agents/*.agent.md` frontmatter.
Treat each agent file as the source of truth for permitted subagents.
Max subagent depth is 3 (configurable via `{{SUBAGENT_MAX_DEPTH}}`).

Current agents:

- Surfaced coordinators: Setup, Code, Review, Fast, Explore, Commit.
- Hidden specialists: Planner, Docs, Debugger, Audit, Researcher, Extensions, Organise.

Exact handoff permissions can evolve. Read `.github/agents/*.agent.md` frontmatter for authoritative `agents:` allow-lists.

## Cross-tool compatibility

- `CLAUDE.md` template stub for Claude Code compatibility
- VS Code settings template at `template/vscode/settings.json`
- Agent plugins enabled via `chat.plugins.enabled`

## Validation

- Main suite: `bash tests/run-all.sh`
- Metadata sync: `bash scripts/workspace/sync-workspace-index.sh --check`
- Model registry sync: `bash scripts/sync/sync-models.sh --check`

## Human documentation

See [`README.md`](README.md) for a human-readable overview and quick-start links.
